segments.text: title.
Today I f- is it up?
By accidentally driving my brand new high-end car straight into a swimming pool.
Hey fellow Redditors, let me start this post by saying, I've always been a bit of a car enthusiast.
For as long as I can remember, I've been knee-deep in auto magazines, drooling over the latest models, and tirelessly saving pennies to afford my own automotive splurge.
Well, the day finally came.
Two weeks ago, I purchased a shiny, brand new 2022 Tesla Model S Plaid, the car of my dreams.
A lavish speedster that could bring any auto lover to their knees.
Now, to the today I effucked up part of my story.
After owning the car for literally two days, a good buddy of mine threw a massive pool party.
Not wanting to miss out, I decided to make a grand entrance with the new whip.
You know, impressed the crowd with its sleek design, glistening paint job, and the purr of the electric motor.
Things were all planned, GPS said, and I was ready to roll here's where the real disaster begins.
Upon arrival, I marveled at the string of luxury cars lined up in the driveway.
Taking my spot at the far end seemed like a prime idea.
It was away from the drink happy lot, and more importantly, showed off the car in all its glory.
What I didn't account for, however, was the fact that the driveway sloped down dangerously close to the goddamn swimming pool.
Panicked, I tried to adjust the overly sensitive brake pedals.
I swear, those things should come with a warning label.
You can perhaps guess what happened next.
In a moment of pure, unadulterated dread, the wheels lurched.
Hell broke loose, the crowd gasped and shrieked as my speed demon ricocheted off a stone pillar and plunged nose first into the chlorinated abyss.
The whole party went quiet.
It was as if somebody hit the mute button.
I scrambled out, dripping wet, mortified and utterly embarrassed to have wrecked my dream car, becoming the laughing stock of the century in the process.
As of now, the insurance companies are playing ping-pong over who's to cover the damage.

Meanwhile, I am dealing with a bunch of bloody legalities and a 120k metallic paperweight soaking at the bottom of a swimming pool, effectively turning its advanced electric system into a deep dive marine exploration rig.
To be continued.
So yeah, today I fucked up, guys.
Stay tuned for updates as I attempt to navigate this car-totaling savings-draining misadventure.
Here's hoping my next post will be from anything other than my penny-pinching bus ride to work.
